Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: From the event log we can observe the following

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process starts with OR and then proceeds to MS and then QS These three activities seem to be in a sequence OR to MS to QS

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After QS we have an activity AS However note that in some cases like Case 4 there is no IQC and the assembly is done once and then proceeds to TST In other cases we see a loop between AS and IQC For example in Case 1 AS then IQC then AS again then TST In Case 3 we see multiple IQC and AS in a loop

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So it seems that after QS we have a loop that can be repeated multiple times we do AS then optionally IQC and then if IQC fails or requires rework we go back to AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop is defined as A B meaning we do A then either exit or do B and then A again In our case A could be AS and B could be IQC This fits the loop structure we do AS then if we need to repeat because of quality issues we do IQC and then AS again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That is exactly AS first time then we decide to repeat so we do B which is IQC and then A again AS So that fits

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But what about having multiple IQC in a row For example in Case 3 after AS we have IQC then IQC again then AS This suggests that maybe the loop is not simply between AS and IQC

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Therefore maybe the loop is not between AS and IQC Alternatively maybe there is a nested loop an outer loop that repeats AS and an inner loop that repeats IQC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The inner loop would be a loop on IQC and the outer loop would be A S with the inner loop as the redo part That would allow AS then two IQC then AS again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That matches AS then two IQC then AS Then after that AS we might do inner loop one IQC and then AS again So that matches Case 3

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Case 1 and Case 2 we see only one IQC between two AS In Case 4 we see no IQC at all So that fits the inner loop can be skipped

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So lets model it as after QS we have an outer loop that repeats AS and an inner loop of IQC

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After TST we see that sometimes there is RT and then TST again This suggests a loop between TST and RT Specifically we have TST and if it fails we do RT and then TST again

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is exactly a loop TST RT Because we do TST then either exit to PK or do RT and then TST again

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then after TST when the loop exits we have PK Then we have an optional LB LB may be skipped so that is a choice between LB and a silent activity Then after that we have DP and D These two seem to be in sequence DP to D

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the event log we see that in all cases the activities happen in a total order From the given event log we do not see concurrency

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we might model the entire process as a sequence of phases but with loops and choices inside

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The overall structure is sequential OR to MS to QS to loop of AS and IQC to loop of TST and RT to PK to optional LB to DP to D

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can have a StrictPartialOrder that contains the following nodes in order OR MS QS the outer loop for AS and IQC the loop for TST and RT PK an XOR for LB DP and D

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we add edges in the StrictPartialOrder to enforce the sequence OR to MS MS to QS QS to outer loop outer loop to testing loop testing loop to PK PK to XOR labeling XOR labeling to DP DP to D

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The inner loop for IQC is inner loop IQC as loop of IQC and skip The outer loop for AS is loop of AS and inner loop IQC The TST loop is loop of TST and RT The optional labeling is XOR of LB and skip

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Case 4 there is no IQC and no RT Our model allows that because the inner loop can be skipped and the testing loop is executed only once Also the labeling can be skipped

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In cases with multiple IQC the inner loop can be repeated multiple times So this model seems to capture the behavior
